title: East Rutherford, New Jersey

infobox.value: Jeffrey Lahullier ([D](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)"), term ends December 31, 2027)[[3]](#cite_note-Mayor-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
295th of 565 in state 20th of 70 in county[[1]](#cite_note-CensusArea-1)

is a [borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_Jersey) "Borough (New Jersey)") in [Bergen County](/wiki/Bergen_County,_New_Jersey "Bergen County, New Jersey"), in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). It is an [inner-ring suburb](/wiki/Inner_suburb "Inner suburb") of [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), located 7 miles (11 km) west of [Midtown Manhattan](/wiki/Midtown_Manhattan "Midtown Manhattan"). As of the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the borough's population was 10,022,[[9]](#cite_note-Census2020-9)[[10]](#cite_note-LWD2020-10) an increase of 1,109 (+12.4%) from the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") count of 8,913,[[19]](#cite_note-Census2010-19)[[20]](#cite_note-LWD2010-20) which in turn reflected an increase of 197 (+2.3%) from the 8,716 counted in the [2000 census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census").[[21]](#cite_note-21)

[American Dream Meadowlands](/wiki/American_Dream_Meadowlands "American Dream Meadowlands") shopping and entertainment complex hosts approximately 200 commercial tenants as of December 2021.[[18]](#cite_note-AmericanDreamEastRutherford-18)

**East Rutherford** is a [borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_Jersey) "Borough (New Jersey)") in [Bergen County](/wiki/Bergen_County,_New_Jersey "Bergen County, New Jersey"), in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey"). It is an [inner-ring suburb](/wiki/Inner_suburb "Inner suburb") of [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), located 7 miles (11 km) west of [Midtown Manhattan](/wiki/Midtown_Manhattan "Midtown Manhattan"). As of the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the borough's population was 10,022,[[9]](#cite_note-Census2020-9)[[10]](#cite_note-LWD2020-10) an increase of 1,109 (+12.4%) from the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") count of 8,913,[[19]](#cite_note-Census2010-19)[[20]](#cite_note-LWD2010-20) which in turn reflected an increase of 197 (+2.3%) from the 8,716 counted in the [2000 census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census").[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Under the terms of an act of the [New Jersey Legislature](/wiki/New_Jersey_Legislature "New Jersey Legislature") on April 17, 1889, a portion of the old [Union Township](/wiki/Union_Township,_Bergen_County,_New_Jersey "Union Township, Bergen County, New Jersey") was incorporated under the name of Boiling Springs Township.[[22]](#cite_note-Story-22) The new township took its name from a spring in the community. On March 28, 1894, the Borough of East Rutherford was created, based on the results of a referendum held the previous day, and Boiling Springs Township was dissolved.[[22]](#cite_note-Story-22) While there was no change in its borders, the name and form of government were changed.[[22]](#cite_note-Story-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) The borough was the second formed during the "[Boroughitis](/wiki/Boroughitis "Boroughitis")" phenomenon then sweeping through Bergen County, in which 26 boroughs were formed in the county in 1894 alone.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

East Rutherford is the home of the [Meadowlands Sports Complex](/wiki/Meadowlands_Sports_Complex "Meadowlands Sports Complex"), which includes [Meadowlands Arena](/wiki/Meadowlands_Arena "Meadowlands Arena") and [MetLife Stadium](/wiki/MetLife_Stadium "MetLife Stadium"). MetLife Stadium is home of the [New York Giants](/wiki/New_York_Giants "New York Giants") and [New York Jets](/wiki/New_York_Jets "New York Jets") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") and hosted [Super Bowl XLVIII](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XLVIII "Super Bowl XLVIII"), which made East Rutherford the smallest city ever to host a [Super Bowl](/wiki/Super_Bowl "Super Bowl"). East Rutherford will be one of 16 venues chosen to host games for the [2026 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup "2026 FIFA World Cup"), with eight matches including the [World Cup final](/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_final "2026 FIFA World Cup final") at MetLife Stadium.

The borough is also the site of [American Dream Meadowlands](/wiki/American_Dream_Meadowlands "American Dream Meadowlands"), a large shopping center and entertainment complex that was originally named "Xanadu" which opened on October 25, 2019.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the borough had a total area of 4.04 square miles (10.47 km2), including 3.71 square miles (9.61 km2) of land and 0.33 square miles (0.85 km2) of water (8.14%).[[1]](#cite_note-CensusArea-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GR1-2)

East Rutherford is bounded on the north by the boroughs of [Carlstadt](/wiki/Carlstadt,_New_Jersey "Carlstadt, New Jersey") and [Wallington](/wiki/Wallington,_New_Jersey "Wallington, New Jersey") and to the south by the borough of [Rutherford](/wiki/Rutherford,_New_Jersey "Rutherford, New Jersey") in Bergen County; by [Secaucus](/wiki/Secaucus,_New_Jersey "Secaucus, New Jersey") in [Hudson County](/wiki/Hudson_County,_New_Jersey "Hudson County, New Jersey"); and by [Passaic](/wiki/Passaic,_New_Jersey "Passaic, New Jersey") in [Passaic County](/wiki/Passaic_County,_New_Jersey "Passaic County, New Jersey").[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28) The [Passaic River](/wiki/Passaic_River "Passaic River") is the western boundary, and the [Hackensack River](/wiki/Hackensack_River "Hackensack River") is the eastern boundary. The area in which East Rutherford is located is the valley of the Passaic and Hackensack rivers.

[Carlton Hill](/wiki/Carlton_Hill,_New_Jersey "Carlton Hill, New Jersey") is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Local_government_in_New_Jersey#Unincorporated_communities "Local government in New Jersey") located within the township.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

The [2010 United States census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") counted 8,913 people, 3,792 households, and 2,226 families in the borough. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 2,403.2 per square mile (927.9/km2). There were 4,018 housing units at an average density of 1,083.4 per square mile (418.3/km2). The racial makeup was 73.04% (6,510) [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._census) "White (U.S. census)"), 4.50% (401) [Black or African American](/wiki/Black_(U.S._census) "Black (U.S. census)"), 0.22% (20) [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._census) "Native American (U.S. census)"), 13.93% (1,242) [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._census) "Asian (U.S. census)"), 0.03% (3) [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. census)"), 5.83% (520) from [other races](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_census "Race and ethnicity in the United States census"), and 2.43% (217) from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._census) "Hispanic (U.S. census)") of any race were 17.54% (1,563) of the population.[[19]](#cite_note-Census2010-19)

Of the 3,792 households, 24.2% had children under the age of 18; 42.9% were married couples living together; 11.6% had a female householder with no husband present and 41.3% were non-families. Of all households, 33.5% were made up of individuals and 11.6% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.35 and the average family size was 3.06.[[19]](#cite_note-Census2010-19)

18.1% of the population were under the age of 18, 8.6% from 18 to 24, 33.2% from 25 to 44, 26.6% from 45 to 64, and 13.5% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 37.8 years. For every 100 females, the population had 93.0 males. For every 100 females ages 18 and older there were 92.5 males.[[19]](#cite_note-Census2010-19)

The Census Bureau's 2006–2010 [American Community Survey](/wiki/American_Community_Survey "American Community Survey") showed that (in 2010 [inflation-adjusted](/wiki/Inflation_adjustment "Inflation adjustment") dollars) [median household income](/wiki/Median_household_income "Median household income") was $62,471 (with a margin of error of +/− $8,225) and the median family income was $71,357 (+/− $10,225). Males had a median income of $57,511 (+/− $8,669) versus $48,502 (+/− $2,269) for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the borough was $32,467 (+/− $2,752). About 5.9% of families and 6.5% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 4.2% of those under age 18 and 13.4% of those age 65 or over.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

[Same-sex couples](/wiki/Domestic_partnership "Domestic partnership") headed 19 households in 2010, a decline from the 27 counted in 2000.[[42]](#cite_note-42)

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [2000 United States census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census"),[[15]](#cite_note-GR2-15) there were 8,716 people, 3,644 households, and 2,157 families residing in the borough. The population density was 2,289.1 inhabitants per square mile (883.8/km2). There were 3,771 housing units at an average density of 990.4 per square mile (382.4/km2). The racial makeup of the borough was 79.68% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 3.72% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.11% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 10.69% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.05% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 3.21% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 2.54% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 10.65% of the population.[[36]](#cite_note-Census2000-36)[[37]](#cite_note-Census2000SF1-37)

There were 3,644 households, out of which 25.2% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 44.4% were married couples living together, 10.8% had a female householder with no husband present, and 40.8% were non-families. 33.4% of all households were made up of individuals, and 11.6% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.35 and the average family size was 3.05.[[36]](#cite_note-Census2000-36)[[37]](#cite_note-Census2000SF1-37)

In the borough, the age distribution of the population shows 19.4% under the age of 18, 7.1% from 18 to 24, 36.5% from 25 to 44, 22.7% from 45 to 64, and 14.3% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 38 years. For every 100 females, there were 94.8 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 93.8 males.[[36]](#cite_note-Census2000-36)[[37]](#cite_note-Census2000SF1-37) The median income for a household in the borough was $50,163, and the median income for a family was $59,583. Males had a median income of $40,798 versus $36,047 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the borough was $28,072. About 7.4% of families and 9.6% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 10.1% of those under age 18 and 11.6% of those age 65 or over.[[36]](#cite_note-Census2000-36)[[37]](#cite_note-Census2000SF1-37)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Economy")]

East Rutherford is home to the [Hudson Group](/wiki/Hudson_Group "Hudson Group"), a retailer which operates a chain of newsstands, bookstores, [fast food](/wiki/Fast_food "Fast food") restaurants, and other retail stores chiefly at [airports](/wiki/Airport "Airport") and train stations.[[43]](#cite_note-43) Contract manufacturing organization [Cambrex Corporation](/wiki/Cambrex_Corporation "Cambrex Corporation") is based in East Rutherford.

The [East Rutherford Operations Center](/wiki/East_Rutherford_Operations_Center "East Rutherford Operations Center") handles currency in the area covered by the [Federal Reserve Bank of New York](/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Bank_of_New_York "Federal Reserve Bank of New York"), a task that includes removing and destroying 5 million currency notes every day.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

The [Dawn Bible Students Association](/wiki/Dawn_Bible_Students_Association "Dawn Bible Students Association") moved to East Rutherford in 1944. The organization has a worldwide outreach and publishes many Bible-based books as well as *The Dawn* magazine.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sports")]

East Rutherford is the home of the [Meadowlands Sports Complex](/wiki/Meadowlands_Sports_Complex "Meadowlands Sports Complex"), which includes [Meadowlands Arena](/wiki/Meadowlands_Arena "Meadowlands Arena") and [MetLife Stadium](/wiki/MetLife_Stadium "MetLife Stadium"), and used to be the location of [Giants Stadium](/wiki/Giants_Stadium "Giants Stadium"). The arena is best known as the former home of the [New Jersey Devils](/wiki/New_Jersey_Devils "New Jersey Devils") of the [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League") and of the [New Jersey Nets](/wiki/Brooklyn_Nets "Brooklyn Nets") of the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association"), and for hosting college basketball, arena football, concerts, and other events. MetLife Stadium is home of the [New York Giants](/wiki/New_York_Giants "New York Giants") and [New York Jets](/wiki/New_York_Jets "New York Jets") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL) and hosted [Super Bowl XLVIII](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XLVIII "Super Bowl XLVIII"), which made East Rutherford the smallest city ever to host a [Super Bowl](/wiki/Super_Bowl "Super Bowl"); it was the former home of the [New York Guardians](/wiki/New_York_Guardians "New York Guardians") of the [XFL](/wiki/XFL_(2020) "XFL (2020)"). East Rutherford will be one of 16 venues chosen to host games for the [2026 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup "2026 FIFA World Cup"), with eight matches including the [final](/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_final "2026 FIFA World Cup final") at MetLife Stadium (which for the purposes of the event will be identified as New York/New Jersey Stadium).[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47) Giants Stadium, which hosted the Giants and Jets until 2009, was the original home of the [New York Red Bulls](/wiki/New_York_Red_Bulls "New York Red Bulls") of [Major League Soccer](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer") from 1996 (founded as the MetroStars) until they moved in 2010 to [Sports Illustrated Stadium](/wiki/Sports_Illustrated_Stadium "Sports Illustrated Stadium") in [Harrison, New Jersey](/wiki/Harrison,_New_Jersey "Harrison, New Jersey").[[48]](#cite_note-48) East Rutherford is the only municipality with fewer than 10,000 residents to have been home to five professional sports teams simultaneously,[[49]](#cite_note-49) as well as the smallest city to host any professional sports team within its city limits, with a population of around 10,000.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Government")]

### Local government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Local government")]

East Rutherford is governed under the [borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_Jersey) "Borough (New Jersey)") form of New Jersey municipal government, which is used in 218 municipalities (of the 564) statewide, making it the most common form of government in New Jersey.[[51]](#cite_note-51) The governing body is comprised of a mayor and a borough council, with all positions elected [at-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large") on a partisan basis as part of the November general election. A mayor is elected directly by the voters to a four-year term of office. The borough council includes six members elected to serve three-year terms on a staggered basis, with two seats coming up for election each year in a three-year cycle.[[6]](#cite_note-DataBook-6) The borough form of government used by East Rutherford is a "[weak mayor](/wiki/Weak_mayor "Weak mayor") / strong council" government in which council members act as the legislative body with the mayor presiding at meetings and voting only in the event of a tie. The mayor can [veto](/wiki/Veto "Veto") ordinances subject to an [override](/wiki/Veto_override "Veto override") by a two-thirds majority vote of the council. The mayor makes committee and liaison assignments for council members, and most appointments are made by the mayor with the advice and consent of the council.[[52]](#cite_note-52)[[53]](#cite_note-53)

As of 2026[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit), East Rutherford's [Mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") is [Democrat](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") Jeffrey Lahullier, whose term of office ends December 31, 2027.[[3]](#cite_note-Mayor-3) The borough council members are Council President Antonio Segalini (D, 2026), Daniel Alvarez Jr. (D, 2027), George W. Cronk (D, 2028), Jesse L. De Rosa (D, 2026), Michael C. Lorusso (D, 2027) and Dennis E. Monks (D, 2028).[[54]](#cite_note-54)[[55]](#cite_note-55)[[56]](#cite_note-BergenCountyDirectory-56)[[57]](#cite_note-Bergen2025-57)[[58]](#cite_note-Bergen2024-58)[[59]](#cite_note-Bergen2023-59)

East Rutherford is located in the 9th Congressional District[[60]](#cite_note-PCR2012-60) and is part of New Jersey's 36th state legislative district.[[61]](#cite_note-Districts2011-61)[[62]](#cite_note-LWV2019-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63)

New Jersey is represented in the [United States Senate](/wiki/United_States_Senate "United States Senate") by [Democrats](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") [Cory Booker](/wiki/Cory_Booker "Cory Booker") ([Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_Jersey "Newark, New Jersey"), term ends 2027) and [Andy Kim](/wiki/Andy_Kim "Andy Kim") ([Moorestown](/wiki/Moorestown,_New_Jersey "Moorestown, New Jersey"), term ends 2031).[[65]](#cite_note-65) For the [2026–2027 session](/wiki/222nd_New_Jersey_Legislature "222nd New Jersey Legislature"), the [36th legislative district](/wiki/New_Jersey%27s_36th_legislative_district "New Jersey's 36th legislative district") of the [New Jersey Legislature](/wiki/New_Jersey_Legislature "New Jersey Legislature") is represented in the [State Senate](/wiki/New_Jersey_Senate "New Jersey Senate") by [Paul Sarlo](/wiki/Paul_Sarlo "Paul Sarlo") ([D](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)"), [Wood-Ridge](/wiki/Wood-Ridge,_New_Jersey "Wood-Ridge, New Jersey")) and in the [General Assembly](/wiki/New_Jersey_General_Assembly "New Jersey General Assembly") by [Clinton Calabrese](/wiki/Clinton_Calabrese "Clinton Calabrese") (D, [Cliffside Park](/wiki/Cliffside_Park,_New_Jersey "Cliffside Park, New Jersey")) and [Gary Schaer](/wiki/Gary_Schaer "Gary Schaer") (D, [Passaic](/wiki/Passaic,_New_Jersey "Passaic, New Jersey")).[[66]](#cite_note-66)

[Bergen County](/wiki/Bergen_County,_New_Jersey "Bergen County, New Jersey") is governed by a directly elected [County Executive](/wiki/Bergen_County_Executive "Bergen County Executive"), with legislative functions performed by a [Board of County Commissioners](/wiki/Board_of_County_Commissioners_(New_Jersey) "Board of County Commissioners (New Jersey)") comprised of seven members who are elected [at-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large") to three-year terms in partisan elections on a staggered basis, with either two or three seats coming up for election each November; a Chairman and Vice Chairman are selected from among its seven members at a reorganization meeting held every January. ## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Education")]

Further information: [East Rutherford School District](/wiki/East_Rutherford_School_District "East Rutherford School District")

Public school students in [pre-kindergarten](/wiki/Pre-kindergarten "Pre-kindergarten") through [eighth grade](/wiki/Eighth_grade "Eighth grade") attend the [East Rutherford School District](/wiki/East_Rutherford_School_District "East Rutherford School District").[[108]](#cite_note-108) As of the 2023–24 school year, the district, comprised of three schools, had an enrollment of 768 students and 81.9 classroom teachers (on an [FTE](/wiki/Full-time_equivalent "Full-time equivalent") basis), for a [student–teacher ratio](/wiki/Student%E2%80%93teacher_ratio "Student–teacher ratio") of 9.4:1.[[109]](#cite_note-NCES-109) Schools in the district (with 2023–24 enrollment data from the [National Center for Education Statistics](/wiki/National_Center_for_Education_Statistics "National Center for Education Statistics")[[110]](#cite_note-110)) are
McKenzie School[[111]](#cite_note-111) with 376 students in grades PreK–3,
Lincoln School[[112]](#cite_note-112) with 153 students in grades 4–5,
Alfred S. Faust School[[113]](#cite_note-113) with 232 students in grades 6–8.[[114]](#cite_note-114)[[115]](#cite_note-115)

For grades [ninth](/wiki/Ninth_grade "Ninth grade") through [twelfth grades](/wiki/Twelfth_grade "Twelfth grade"), public school students attend the [Becton Regional High School](/wiki/Becton_Regional_High_School "Becton Regional High School") in East Rutherford, which serves high school students from both [Carlstadt](/wiki/Carlstadt,_New_Jersey "Carlstadt, New Jersey") and East Rutherford as part of the [Carlstadt-East Rutherford Regional School District](/wiki/Carlstadt-East_Rutherford_Regional_School_District "Carlstadt-East Rutherford Regional School District"), together with students from [Maywood](/wiki/Maywood,_New_Jersey "Maywood, New Jersey"), who attend as part of a [sending/receiving relationship](/wiki/Sending/receiving_relationship "Sending/receiving relationship").[[116]](#cite_note-116) As of the 2023–24 school year, the high school had an enrollment of 821 students and 61.5 classroom teachers (on an [FTE](/wiki/Full-time_equivalent "Full-time equivalent") basis), for a [student–teacher ratio](/wiki/Student%E2%80%93teacher_ratio "Student–teacher ratio") of 13.4:1.[[117]](#cite_note-117) Seats on the high school district's ten-member board of education are allocated based on the population of the constituent municipalities, with five seats allocated to East Rutherford.[[118]](#cite_note-118)

Public school students from the borough, and all of Bergen County, are eligible to attend the secondary education programs offered by the [Bergen County Technical Schools](/wiki/Bergen_County_Technical_Schools "Bergen County Technical Schools"), which include the [Bergen County Academies](/wiki/Bergen_County_Academies "Bergen County Academies") in [Hackensack](/wiki/Hackensack,_New_Jersey "Hackensack, New Jersey"), and the [Bergen Tech campus in Teterboro](/wiki/Bergen_County_Technical_High_School,_Teterboro_Campus "Bergen County Technical High School, Teterboro Campus") or [Paramus](/wiki/Bergen_County_Technical_High_School,_Paramus_Campus "Bergen County Technical High School, Paramus Campus"). The district offers programs on a shared-time or full-time basis, with admission based on a selective application process and tuition covered by the student's home school district.[[119]](#cite_note-119)[[120]](#cite_note-120)

## Emergency services

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Emergency services")]

### Police

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Police")]

The East Rutherford Police Department provides emergency and protective services to the borough of East Rutherford.

It consists of a Patrol Division, Detective Bureau, Traffic Division, Juvenile Division, and Records Bureau.

The Chief of Police is Dennis M. Rivelli.[[121]](#cite_note-121)

### Fire

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Fire")]

The East Rutherford Fire Department (ERFD) is an [all-volunteer fire department](/wiki/Volunteer_fire_department "Volunteer fire department"). The ERFD was organized in 1894 and consists of a [chief](/wiki/Fire_Chief "Fire Chief") and three assistant chiefs. There are three [fire stations](/wiki/Fire_station "Fire station"). The department is staffed by eighty fully trained [firefighters](/wiki/Firefighter "Firefighter"). The ERFD utilizes two [Engines](/wiki/Fire_apparatus#Fire_engine "Fire apparatus"), a [Ladder truck](/wiki/Fire_apparatus#Turntable_ladder "Fire apparatus"), a [Heavy Rescue](/wiki/Heavy_rescue_vehicle "Heavy rescue vehicle"), and a [Quint](/wiki/Fire_apparatus#Quint_and_Quad "Fire apparatus"). The ERFD also provides [emergency medical service](/wiki/Emergency_medical_services "Emergency medical services") to the borough.[[122]](#cite_note-122)

* Engine 1 2008 Sutphen 1750/750/20F
* Engine 2 2008 Sutphen 1750/750/20F
* Engine 3 2008 Sutphen 2000/500/75' Midmount Quint
* Ladder 1 2008 Sutphen 2000/300/100' Aerial Tower
* Rescue 4 2010 International/Sutphen 500/300/20F

### Public transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Public transportation")]

[Rutherford station](/wiki/Rutherford_station "Rutherford station"),[[124]](#cite_note-124) which is located on the [Rutherford](/wiki/Rutherford,_New_Jersey "Rutherford, New Jersey") – East Rutherford border, provides train service on [NJ Transit](/wiki/NJ_Transit "NJ Transit")'s [Bergen County Line](/wiki/Bergen_County_Line "Bergen County Line").[[125]](#cite_note-125) The [Meadowlands station](/wiki/Meadowlands_station "Meadowlands station")[[126]](#cite_note-126) offers service on the [Meadowlands Rail Line](/wiki/Meadowlands_Rail_Line "Meadowlands Rail Line"), which began in June 2009, providing access between the Meadowlands Sports Complex and [Secaucus Junction](/wiki/Secaucus_Junction "Secaucus Junction"), and from there to other NJ Transit lines with trains operating before and after games and other events at the complex.[[127]](#cite_note-127)

NJ Transit buses include the [160](/wiki/160_(New_Jersey_bus) "160 (New Jersey bus)"), [163](/wiki/163_(New_Jersey_bus) "163 (New Jersey bus)"), and [164](/wiki/164_(New_Jersey_bus) "164 (New Jersey bus)") routes serving the [Port Authority Bus Terminal](/wiki/Port_Authority_Bus_Terminal "Port Authority Bus Terminal") in [Midtown Manhattan](/wiki/Midtown_Manhattan "Midtown Manhattan"); the [76](/wiki/76_(New_Jersey_bus) "76 (New Jersey bus)") to [Newark](/wiki/Newark,_New_Jersey "Newark, New Jersey"); and local service on the [703](/wiki/703_(New_Jersey_bus) "703 (New Jersey bus)") route.[[128]](#cite_note-128)[[129]](#cite_note-129)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Notable people")]

See also: [Category:People from East Rutherford, New Jersey](/wiki/Category:People_from_East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey "Category:People from East Rutherford, New Jersey")

People who were born in, residents of, or otherwise closely associated with East Rutherford include:

* [Carol Arthur](/wiki/Carol_Arthur "Carol Arthur") (1935–2020), actress, mainly recognizable as playing supporting roles in films produced by [Mel Brooks](/wiki/Mel_Brooks "Mel Brooks")[[130]](#cite_note-130)
* [E. J. Barthel](/wiki/E._J._Barthel "E. J. Barthel") (born 1985), [fullback](/wiki/Fullback_(American_football) "Fullback (American football)") who played for the [Las Vegas Locomotives](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Locomotives "Las Vegas Locomotives") of the [United Football League](/wiki/United_Football_League_(2009%E2%80%9312) "United Football League (2009–12)")[[131]](#cite_note-131)
* [Ernest Cuneo](/wiki/Ernest_Cuneo "Ernest Cuneo") (1905–1988), lawyer, newspaperman, author and intelligence liaison, who played two seasons in the NFL for the [Orange Tornadoes](/wiki/Orange_Tornadoes "Orange Tornadoes") and the [Brooklyn Dodgers](/wiki/Brooklyn_Dodgers_(NFL) "Brooklyn Dodgers (NFL)")[[132]](#cite_note-132)
* [Fireman Ed](/wiki/Fireman_Ed "Fireman Ed") (born 1959, nickname of Edwin M. Anzalone), superfan of the [New York Jets](/wiki/New_York_Jets "New York Jets")[[133]](#cite_note-133)
* [Alfred Byrd Graf](/wiki/Alfred_Byrd_Graf "Alfred Byrd Graf") (1901–2001), botanist known for his richly illustrated books on the subject of plants[[134]](#cite_note-134)
* [Henry Helstoski](/wiki/Henry_Helstoski "Henry Helstoski") (1925–1999), represented [New Jersey's 9th congressional district](/wiki/New_Jersey%27s_9th_congressional_district "New Jersey's 9th congressional district"), served as councilman of East Rutherford in 1956 and as mayor from 1957 to 1965[[135]](#cite_note-135)
* [Harold C. Hollenbeck](/wiki/Harold_C._Hollenbeck "Harold C. Hollenbeck") (born 1938), politician who represented New Jersey in the [United States House of Representatives](/wiki/United_States_House_of_Representatives "United States House of Representatives") from 1977 to 1983[[136]](#cite_note-136)
* [Henry Hook](/wiki/Henry_Hook_(crossword_compiler) "Henry Hook (crossword compiler)") (1955–2015), [crossword](/wiki/Crossword "Crossword") creator[[137]](#cite_note-137)
* [Bobby Jones](/wiki/Bobby_Jones_(left-handed_pitcher) "Bobby Jones (left-handed pitcher)") (born 1972), former pitcher who played for the [New York Mets](/wiki/New_York_Mets "New York Mets")[[138]](#cite_note-138)
* [Martin Kilson](/wiki/Martin_Kilson "Martin Kilson") (1931–2019), political scientist who was the first black academic to be appointed a full professor at [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University")[[139]](#cite_note-139)
* [Jim Powers](/wiki/Jim_Powers "Jim Powers") (born 1958), retired [professional wrestler](/wiki/Professional_wrestling "Professional wrestling") best known for his appearances with the [World Wrestling Federation](/wiki/WWE "WWE") from 1987 to 1994[[140]](#cite_note-140)
* [Diane Ruggiero](/wiki/Diane_Ruggiero "Diane Ruggiero") (born 1970), screenwriter for *[Veronica Mars](/wiki/Veronica_Mars "Veronica Mars")*[[141]](#cite_note-141)
* [Patty Shwartz](/wiki/Patty_Shwartz "Patty Shwartz") (born 1961), is a United States Circuit Judge of [United States Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit](/wiki/United_States_Court_of_Appeals_for_the_Third_Circuit "United States Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit")[[142]](#cite_note-142)
* [Dick Vitale](/wiki/Dick_Vitale "Dick Vitale") (born 1939), sports broadcaster who attended high school and coached at his alma mater, East Rutherford High School; inducted into the East Rutherford Hall of Fame in 1985[[143]](#cite_note-143)
